data.title: Coinbase Chief Legal Officer: "Clear Bill" is "very close" to reaching an agreement on stablecoin yields.
data.description: In an interview with Fox Business, Coinbase Chief Legal Officer Paul Grewal stated that the debate surrounding stablecoin yields in the upcoming Clarity Act is "very close" to reaching an agreement. Grewal said the crypto market structure bill is progressing, although the debate surrounding stablecoin yields continues. He noted that parties are gradually recognizing that while reward mechanisms are important, other key elements of the bill are equally crucial to realizing President Trump's vision of making the US the "global crypto capital." The US banking industry has been lobbying for the Clarity Act to include provisions prohibiting crypto platforms from offering yields on idle stablecoin balances, arguing that this could lead to a significant outflow of bank deposits. Grewal stated that there is currently no evidence of an actual outflow of deposits and pointed out that the stablecoin yield issue should not be confused with other challenges facing the banking industry. Grewal expressed optimism about the bill's prospects, saying he expects the Senate Banking Committee to begin hearings in the coming weeks and ultimately hold a full vote. Coinbase and its CEO Brian Armstrong have repeatedly publicly opposed bills that would ban rewards for idle stablecoin balances, arguing that such restrictions would stifle innovation in the United States and harm consumers. Meanwhile, Coinbase's stock price (COIN) has fallen 50% over the past six months amid a continued downturn in the crypto market, closing down 0.9% at $172.99 on Wednesday.

data.title: Australian Miners Face Production Shortfalls Due to Severe Weather, Analysts Say
data.description: Barrenjoey analysts have indicated that Australian mining companies may collectively report production shortfalls this fiscal quarter due to adverse weather conditions. According to Jin10, the Australian bank anticipates that companies such as BHP, Rio Tinto, Fortescue, South32, and Mineral Resources will perform below expectations due to disruptions caused by end-of-quarter cyclones and other weather-related issues. Additionally, Coronado, Evolution, Sandfire, and IGO are also expected to record production gaps. However, Barrenjoey notes that Lynas, a rare earth miner, may exceed revenue expectations thanks to stronger-than-expected prices. Furthermore, Vault Minerals is projected to outperform in cash flow as it settles most of its hedge positions by the end of 2025.

data.title: South Korea Delays Digital Asset Legislation Discussions Until Post-Election
data.description: South Korea's National Policy Committee has postponed discussions on the second phase of its digital asset basic act until after the local elections on June 3. According to NS3.AI, the committee removed the bill from its bill review subcommittee agenda on March 31. This delay leaves unresolved issues regarding stablecoin issuance rules and potential restrictions on major exchange shareholders' equity holdings.

data.title: Tokyo Stock Market Reverses Gains Following Trump's Speech
data.description: The Tokyo stock market experienced a downturn after initially showing gains. According to RTHK, the Nikkei Index rose nearly 520 points earlier in the morning, reaching 54,258 points. However, following a televised speech by U.S. President Donald Trump, the market shifted direction. By midday, the index had fallen by 1,007 points, closing at 52,731 points, marking a decline of 1.88%.

data.title: Expert: Trump's Speech Fails to Boost Market Confidence Amid War Concerns
data.description: Vaneck's Head of Investments and Capital Markets, Russel Chesler, commented on April 2 that U.S. President Donald Trump's recent speech did not positively impact market sentiment. According to Jin10, Chesler noted that if Trump's intention was to bolster market confidence, he was unsuccessful. The prevailing concern among investors is the uncertainty surrounding the duration of the ongoing war, which is contributing to market volatility. As the market anticipates a prolonged conflict, asset prices are beginning to retract. In this highly volatile environment, the U.S. dollar is experiencing short-term strength. However, Chesler emphasized that the long-term trend of structural weakening for the dollar remains unchanged. The current market situation is characterized by slowing economic growth coupled with rising inflation expectations, leading towards stagflation.

data.title: Solana's Meteora Assures Safety of Funds Amid Drift Protocol Concerns
data.description: Solana's liquidity protocol, Meteora, has announced that all funds on its platform are secure. According to Foresight News, Meteora clarified that its platform's functions and vaults have not interacted with the Drift protocol, ensuring the safety of user assets.

data.title: Crypto markets generally declined, but the ETH and GameFi sectors remained relatively resilient.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, the crypto market generally declined. The RWA sector fell 1.71% in the last 24 hours, with Ondo Finance (ONDO) down 3.42% and Keeta (KTA) down 7.24%. The Layer 1 sector fell 1.66%, with Solana (SOL) down 4.30% and Canton Network (CC) down 3.96%. Additionally, Bitcoin (BTC) fell 0.75%, dropping below $68,000. However, Ethereum (ETH) rose 0.15%, remaining above $2,000. In other sectors, the GameFi sector remained relatively strong, rising 0.71% in the last 24 hours, with Gomining Token (GMT) up 4.61%; the Meme sector fell 0.51%, but MemeCore (M) surged 5.79% intraday; the Layer 2 sector fell 0.63%, with ImmutableX (IMX) down 4.25%; the DeFi sector fell 0.70%, with Ethena (ENA) down 4.78%; the PayFi sector fell 0.72%, with Telcoin (TEL) bucking the trend and rising 7.50%; the CeFi sector fell 1.65%, with Binance Coin (BNB) down 2.58%. Crypto sector indices reflecting historical sector performance show that the ssiAI and ssiSocialFi indices rose 0.82% and 0.08% respectively, while the ssiCeFi index fell 1.77%.

data.title: Bitcoin Demand Declines as Whale Addresses Shift to Selling
data.description: Bitcoin's demand experienced a significant downturn, turning negative by approximately 63,000 BTC at the end of March. According to NS3.AI, CryptoQuant reported that whale addresses, which had been steadily accumulating Bitcoin, shifted their strategy to net selling. This change in behavior among large holders has raised concerns about the cryptocurrency's market dynamics.

data.title: Orca CEO Assures User Funds Are Safe Following Drift Attack
data.description: Solana liquidity protocol Orca's CEO, Michael Hwang, has stated that Orca remains unaffected by the recent Drift attack, ensuring the safety of user funds. According to Foresight News, Hwang emphasized that Orca's smart contracts have been audited by four independent firms, and its operational security measures have recently undergone review by external security consultants.

data.title: The Drift hacker's linked address likely originated from Backpack, or left clues related to KYC verification.
data.description: SendAI developer aryan posted on X: "I found some interesting clues on-chain: The Drift attacker address (HkG...ZES) received funds through Near Intents 8 days ago, but remained inactive until suddenly receiving a large amount of funds from the Drift vault; the attacker then transferred/exchanged these funds to a money laundering address (8ub...Gxw) and several other addresses, all of which received funds through Backpack just yesterday. This is interesting because it means that Backpack may have performed KYC on these accounts; then, these money laundering addresses transferred the funds to an Ethereum address through Wormhole, which had previously received funds through Tornado Cash."

data.description: On Apr 02, 2026, 02:14 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 67,000 USDT and is now trading at 66,943.40625 USDT, with a narrowed 1.15% decrease in 24 hours.

data.description: On Apr 02, 2026, 02:21 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has dropped below 600 USDT and is now trading at 599.51001 USDT, with a narrowed 2.65% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: European Stock Futures Experience Decline
data.description: European stock futures have seen a notable decline. According to ChainCatcher, the Euro Stoxx 50 futures fell by 1.2%, while Germany's DAX futures also decreased by 1.2%. Additionally, the UK's FTSE 100 futures experienced a 0.2% drop.

data.title: Account with over $1.7 million in profits and a total purchase of $160,000. NBA regular season game: Spurs defeat Warriors.
data.description: Odaily Seer's monitoring shows that in the Polymarket "NBA Regular Season Bulls vs. Pacers" prediction event, an account with over $1.7 million in profits (address: 0xbddf61af533ff524d27154e589d2d7a81510c684) purchased $160,000 worth of bets on the Spurs defeating the Warriors. The NBA regular season game between the Spurs and Warriors tipped off at 10:00 AM Beijing time today. The NBA regular season is currently nearing its end; the Spurs have a record of 57 wins and 18 losses, ranking 2nd in the Western Conference; the Warriors have a record of 36 wins and 39 losses, ranking 10th in the Western Conference. Odaily Seer continuously monitors the prediction market, seeing changes before prices are set.

data.title: Yu Xian: Drift migrated its multi-signature service a week ago. The new multi-signature service only requires a 2/5 signature threshold and has no time lock.
data.description: Yu Xian, founder of SlowMist, posted on X: "Drift Protocol suffered a loss of over $200 million. A week ago, they migrated to a 2/5 multisignature pool (1 old + 4 new signers) without timelock. A few hours ago, the attackers took over admin privileges, minted CVT counterfeit coins, manipulated the oracle, disabled relevant security mechanisms, and absconded with the pool's valuable assets..."

data.title: Iran launches missile in response to Trump's speech
data.description: According to Al Jazeera: Just as Trump concluded his national address, Iran launched another large number of missiles at Israel. (Jinshi)

data.title: ZachXBT again criticized Circle for its inaction in the Drift hack, which allowed illicit funds to leak from Solana via CCTP.
data.description: Renowned blockchain analyst ZachXBT has once again criticized Circle, stating that the Drift hack occurred during US trading hours, with millions of USDC being transferred from Solana to Ethereum via CCTP (Circle's cross-chain protocol), while Circle remained dormant and inactive. Just days earlier, Circle had also arbitrarily frozen at least 16 enterprise hot wallets, which are still slowly unfreezing. ZachXBT concluded by saying, "Circle is a bad actor in this industry."

data.title: The whale pension-usdt.eth holds a short position of $92.4 million, with a floating profit of $1.6 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, the whale pension-usdt.eth (0x0ddf...a902) opened a new short position of 20,000 ETH with 3x leverage and increased its short position of BTC with 3x leverage to 750 BTC, with a total value of $92.4 million. Currently, this address has a floating profit of over $1.6 million and a total profit of over $32 million.

data.title: XRP Spot ETF Experiences Significant Outflow
data.description:
According to Odaily, data from SoSoValue indicates that on April 1, Eastern Time, the XRP spot ETF saw a net outflow of $1.3158 million in a single day.

The outflow was solely from the 21Shares XRP ETF (TOXR), which recorded a daily net outflow of $1.3158 million. The historical total net inflow now stands at -$25.1838 million.

As of the time of reporting, the total net asset value of the XRP spot ETF is $948 million, with an XRP net asset ratio of 1.14%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $1.208 billion.

data.title: Most Hong Kong-listed OpenClaw concept stocks fell.
data.description: Most Hong Kong-listed OpenClaw concept stocks declined, with Zhipu (02513.HK) falling over 15%, MINIMAX-W (00100.HK) falling over 9%, Kingsoft Cloud (03896.HK) and Xiaomi Group (01810.HK) falling over 3%, and Alibaba (09988.HK) and Meituan (03690.HK) falling over 2%. (Jinshi)
